title: AME model fitting routine for longitudinal relational data — lame
text: MCMC routine providing fit additive multiplicative effects (AME) regression model longitudinal (time-series) relational data various types. Supports unipartite (square) bipartite (rectangular) network structures. cross-sectional (single time point) networks, use ame function.

title: AME model fitting routine for longitudinal relational data — lame
code: lame(   Y,   Xdyad = NULL,   Xrow = NULL,   Xcol = NULL,   rvar = !(family == "rrl"),   cvar = TRUE,   dcor = !symmetric,   nvar = TRUE,   R = 0,   R_row = NULL,   R_col = NULL,   mode = c("unipartite", "bipartite"),   dynamic_uv = FALSE,   dynamic_ab = FALSE,   dynamic_G = FALSE,   dynamic_beta = FALSE,   dynamic_beta_kind = c("ar1", "rw1", "rw2", "matern32"),   dynamic_uv_kind = c("ar1", "snap", "t"),   family = "normal",   intercept = !is.element(family, c("rrl", "ordinal")),   symmetric = FALSE,   odmax = NULL,   prior = list(),   g = NA,   seed = 6886,   nscan = 10000,   burn = 500,   odens = 25,   plot = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE,   gof = TRUE,   start_vals = NULL,   periodic_save = FALSE,   out_file = NULL,   save_interval = 0.25,   model.name = NULL,   save_log_lik = FALSE,   log_lik_path = NULL,   log_lik_chunk_size = 10000L,   keep_snap_draws = c("none", "summary", "draws", "chunked"),   freeze_call = FALSE,   dynamic_beta_pool = c("none", "rho", "sigma", "both"),   dynamic_beta_per_actor = NULL,   per_actor_covariate_idx = 1L,   per_actor_identifiability = c("center", "exact_center", "drop_population"),   keep_per_actor = c("auto", "draws", "summary", "none"),   time_index = NULL,   period_exposure = NULL,   max_seconds = Inf,   checkpoint_path = NULL,   checkpoint_every = 100L,   log_lik_method = c("observed_exact", "observed_ghk", "augmented"),   ordinal_cutpoints = c("data_induced", "explicit"),   method = c("mcmc", "als"),   als_stability = c("none", "quick", "validation"),   als_max_iter = 200L,   als_tol = NULL,   bootstrap = 0L,   bootstrap_type = c("parametric", "block"),   bootstrap_block_length = 1L,   bootstrap_seed = NULL,   resume_from = NULL,   print )

title: AME model fitting routine for longitudinal relational data — lame
text: Y T length list n x n relational matrices, 3D array dimensions [n, n, T], T corresponds number replicates (time, example). 3D array provided, automatically converted list format. See family various data types. Xdyad T length list n x n x pd arrays covariates Xrow T length list n x pr matrices nodal row covariates Xcol T length list n x pc matrices nodal column covariates rvar logical: fit row random effects (asymmetric case)? cvar logical: fit column random effects (asymmetric case)? dcor logical: fit dyadic correlation (asymmetric case)? nvar logical: fit nodal random effects (symmetric case)? R integer: dimension multiplicative effects (can zero) R_row integer: bipartite networks, dimension row node multiplicative effects (defaults R) R_col integer: bipartite networks, dimension column node multiplicative effects (defaults R) mode character: either "unipartite" (default) square networks "bipartite" rectangular networks dynamic_uv logical: fit dynamic multiplicative effects (latent factors). transition model selected dynamic_uv_kind. Default FALSE. dynamic_ab logical: fit dynamic additive effects (sender/receiver effects) evolve time using AR(1) processes. TRUE, row effects () column effects (b) become time-varying, following \(a_{,t} = \rho_{ab} a_{,t-1} + \epsilon_{,t}\). captures temporal heterogeneity actors' baseline propensities send receive ties, allowing smooth changes activity levels popularity time. example, actor's tendency form outgoing ties might gradually increase decrease across observation periods. AR(1) specification ensures temporal smoothness allowing actor-specific evolution patterns. Implementation uses conjugate updates possible C++ computational efficiency. Default FALSE. dynamic_G logical (bipartite ). TRUE bipartite interaction matrix \(G_t\) \(U_t G_t V_t'\) varies period returned fit$G_cube, \(R_\text{row} \times R_\text{col} \times T\) array. MCMC fits also return posterior summaries cube; ALS fits return penalized point path using storage names. marginal bilinear predictor identified package's canonicalization conventions, individual entries \(G_t\) can move equivalent rotations scalings \(U_t\) \(V_t\). Default FALSE. dynamic_beta logical, character, integer, logical-vector flag selecting regression coefficients evolve time via independent AR(1) processes. Default FALSE keeps every coefficient static (historical behaviour). Accepted forms: FALSE / NULL: coefficient dynamic. TRUE: every coefficient dynamic. character vector block shortcuts ("intercept", "dyad", "row", "col") specific coefficient names colnames(BETA) — become dynamic. integer vector: 1-based column indices colnames(BETA). logical vector length p: per-coefficient mask. dynamic-coefficient block (one per distinct intercept / dyad / row / col label least one dynamic coefficient) gets AR(1) parameters \(\rho_\beta\) (truncated-Normal prior, default mean 0.8, bounded 0 0.999) \(\sigma_\beta^2\) (inverse-Gamma prior, defaults shape 2 / scale 1). joint posterior time path \(\beta_t\) drawn Forward-Filter / Backward-Sample (FFBS) inside MCMC loop, conditional current static-block beta (, b, U, V). intercept nodal coefficient dynamic, sum--zero contrast basis applied additive-effect sampler keep \(a_i + intercept_t\) identified. Requires least 2 time periods. method = "als", selected intercept, dyadic, row-node, column-node coefficients supported normal, binary, Poisson panels, including named panels actors enter exit. Dynamic ALS uses period-specific Xrow Xcol values selected dynamic node coefficients; static node coefficients still use per-actor means. Dynamic intercepts unavailable family = "ordinal" "rrl" (row-nodal coefficients unavailable family = "rrl"). Default FALSE. dynamic_beta_kind character: state-space prior dynamic coefficient block(s). "ar1" (default) gives mean-reverting AR(1) truncated-Normal prior \(\rho_\beta\). "rw1" gives random walk (\(\rho_\beta\) pinned 1, truncation), appropriate expect permanent drift mean reversion – e.g. trade-gravity coefficients permanently changing world economy. alias "random_walk" also accepted "rw1". stationarity warning summary(fit) fires default AR(1), refit dynamic_beta_kind = "rw1" cleaner inference. Decision tree: mean-reverting? AR(1). Permanent / unit-root drift? RW1. Smooth curvature? "rw2" (second-order random walk). Smooth known length-scale? "matern32" (Matern 3/2). Note: "rw2" "matern32" use R-level joint Gaussian sampler run substantially slower per iteration C++ FFBS used "ar1" / "rw1" (roughly 2-4x n = 100, T = 10). dynamic_uv_kind Character string selecting transition model dynamic multiplicative latent positions dynamic_uv = TRUE. "ar1" uses Gaussian AR(1) drift, "snap" uses mixture AR(1) drift discontinuous reset transitions, "t" uses heavy-tailed Student-t innovations represented local transition scales. MCMC path, "snap" "t" currently supported unipartite directed symmetric models . method = "als", "snap" also supported normal bipartite panels dynamic block \(G\) static. method = "als", "t" supported bipartite normal, binary, Poisson panels. ALS Student-t dynamic UV fits attach final local transition-weight matrices fit$lambda_u fit$lambda_v. family character: one "normal","tobit","binary","ordinal","cbin","frn","rrl","poisson" - see details intercept logical: fit model intercept? symmetric logical: sociomatrix symmetric? odmax scalar integer vector length n giving maximum number nominations node may make - used "frn" "cbin" families prior list containing hyperparameters prior distributions. Available options defaults: Sab0 Prior covariance matrix additive effects (default: diag(2)). 2x2 matrix Sab0\[1,1\] prior variance row effects, Sab0\[2,2\] prior variance column effects, -diagonals control correlation row column effects. eta0 Prior degrees freedom covariance multiplicative effects (default: 4 + 3 \* n/100, n number actors). Higher values impose stronger shrinkage latent factors. etaab Prior degrees freedom covariance additive effects (default: 4 + 3 \* n/100). Controls shrinkage row/column random effects. rho_uv_mean dynamic_uv=TRUE: Prior mean UV AR(1) parameter (default: 0.9). Values close 1 indicate high temporal persistence. rho_uv_sd dynamic_uv=TRUE: Prior SD UV AR(1) parameter (default: 0.1). Controls uncertainty temporal dependence. sigma_uv_shape dynamic_uv=TRUE: Shape parameter inverse-gamma prior UV innovation variance (default: 2). sigma_uv_scale dynamic_uv=TRUE: Scale parameter inverse-gamma prior UV innovation variance (default: 1). rho_ab_mean dynamic_ab=TRUE: Prior mean additive effects AR(1) parameter (default: 0.8). Controls temporal smoothness sender/receiver effects. rho_ab_sd dynamic_ab=TRUE: Prior SD additive effects AR(1) parameter (default: 0.15). sigma_ab_shape dynamic_ab=TRUE: Shape parameter inverse-gamma prior additive effects innovation variance (default: 2). sigma_ab_scale dynamic_ab=TRUE: Scale parameter inverse-gamma prior additive effects innovation variance (default: 1). rho_beta_mean dynamic_beta: Prior mean per-block AR(1) parameter time-varying regression coefficients (default: 0.8). Closer 1 = smoother evolution. rho_beta_sd dynamic_beta: Prior SD per-block AR(1) parameter (default: 0.15). rho_beta_lower dynamic_beta: Lower truncation bound AR(1) parameter (default: 0). Pass negative value allow negative autoregression. rho_beta_upper dynamic_beta: Upper truncation bound (default: 0.999). Closer 1 admits near-unit-root behaviour. sigma_beta_shape dynamic_beta: Shape parameter inverse-Gamma prior per-block innovation variance (default: 2). sigma_beta_scale dynamic_beta: Scale parameter inverse-Gamma prior (default: 1). sigma_beta_init dynamic_beta: Initial value per-block innovation standard deviation (default: 0.25). Affects mixing, stationary distribution. beta0_mean dynamic_beta: Mean Gaussian prior dynamic coefficients t = 0 (default: 0). beta0_var dynamic_beta: Variance Gaussian prior dynamic coefficients t = 0 (default: 10). Weakly informative. Common usage: prior = list(Sab0 = diag(c(1, 1)), eta0 = 10) stronger shrinkage, prior = list(rho_uv_mean = 0.95) higher temporal persistence, prior = list(rho_beta_mean = 0.95, sigma_beta_scale = 0.1) smooth time-varying coefficients tight innovations. g optional scalar vector g-prior regression coefficients. Default p^2 p number regression parameters. g-prior controls variance regression coefficients: larger values allow larger coefficient values. Can vector length p parameter-specific control. seed random seed nscan number iterations Markov chain (beyond burn-) burn burn Markov chain odens output density Markov chain plot logical: plot results running? verbose logical: print progress running? Default FALSE. gof logical: calculate goodness fit statistics? start_vals list parameter starting values MCMC chain periodic_save logical: indicating whether periodically save MCMC results out_file character vector indicating name path file stored periodic_save selected. example, Apple OS out_file="~/Desktop/ameFit.rda". save_interval quantile interval indicating save post-burn-period. model.name optional string model selection output save_log_lik one FALSE (default), TRUE, "chunked". TRUE, stores per-iteration pointwise log-likelihood matrix fit$log_lik ([n_stored, n_obs] double matrix). "chunked", streams log-lik values per-column-chunk binary files log_lik_path -memory cost MCMC just one chunk's width; chunks recovered later via read_log_lik(fit). Required loo::loo(fit) loo::waic(fit). log_lik_path directory write log-lik chunks save_log_lik = "chunked". Default NULL creates session-scoped subdirectory tempdir(). log_lik_chunk_size column-width -disk chunk save_log_lik = "chunked". Larger chunks mean fewer files (one readBin per chunk read) memory MCMC. Default 10000L. keep_snap_draws controls storage post-burn-MCMC snap-shift indicators dynamic_uv = TRUE dynamic_uv_kind = "snap". "none" keeps posterior mean snap probabilities. "summary" stores cell-level count, mean, variance summaries. "draws" stores retained binary indicator arrays fit$snap_draws , directed fits, fit$snap_draws_v, dimensions draw x actor x time first period set NA. "chunked" currently stores arrays memory labels storage mode downstream tooling. freeze_call logical: TRUE, store snapshot evaluated Y, Xdyad, Xrow, Xcol fit$data_snapshot later update(fit, ...) refits data even caller mutated objects workspace. Memory cost equals size data; default FALSE. dynamic_beta_pool one "none" (default), "rho", "sigma", "". two coefficient blocks dynamic, non-"none" values update shared hyperparameters across blocks selected persistence / innovation-scale component. single dynamic block value recorded block pool . dynamic_beta_per_actor optional, one NULL (default), "row", "col". non-NULL, fits actor-specific time-varying deviations one dyadic covariate selected margin. default per_actor_identifiability = "center" keeps population coefficient coef(fit) enforces per-period sum--zero constraint actor deviations. Draws stored fit$THETA_ACTOR retained; summary mode stores fit$theta_actor_mean fit$theta_actor_sd. per_actor_covariate_idx positive integer; index dyadic covariate cube slope per-actor extension. Default 1L. per_actor_identifiability one "center" (default) "drop_population". "center" preserves population coefficient constrains per-actor deviations sum zero per period via pairwise contrast FFBS. "drop_population" reserved currently falls back "center". keep_per_actor one "auto" (default; full draws memory cost < 250 MB else streaming summary), "draws" (full [n_iter, n_actors, T] cube), "summary" (streaming posterior mean + sd ), "none" (hyperparameter chains). time_index optional numeric vector length \(T\) giving observation times. Default NULL treats periods equally spaced. Strictly-increasing values required supplied. Unequal gaps scale AR(1) / RW1 transition variance also used RW2 Matérn 3/2 dynamic-beta precision builders. period_exposure optional non-negative numeric vector length \(T\) giving period-level exposure offsets. Wired Poisson: supplied value != 1, Poisson observation likelihood becomes \(Y_{ij,t} \sim \text{Poisson}(e_t \cdot \exp(Z_{ij,t}))\), latent \(Z\) retains existing semantic "unexposed log-rate". families, non-trivial period_exposure error (rescale Y use covariate offset). NULL (default) -ones uses unscaled likelihood path. max_seconds optional positive scalar; MCMC wall-clock time exceeds many seconds, chain terminates cleanly fit$terminated_early set TRUE. checkpoint_path optional file path. set, chain periodically writes snapshot BETA, VC, RNG state, original call file. Use lame_resume pass path back resume_from = path continue. checkpoint_every positive integer; iterations checkpoint writes. Default 100L. log_lik_method one "observed_exact" (default), "observed_ghk", "augmented". Selects argument selecting pointwise log-lik stored fit$log_lik. "observed_exact" uses closed-form marginal log-likelihood normal, binary, cbin, tobit, poisson, ordinal fits. "observed_ghk" uses randomized-Halton GHK Monte Carlo marginal rank-family fits ("frn" "rrl") exact observed likelihood GHK step needed. "augmented" uses augmented-data Gaussian--\(Z\) contribution. ordinal_cutpoints character: cutpoint convention family = "ordinal". "data_induced" (default) uses data-induced cutpoints; "explicit" samples explicit cutpoints via Cowles (1996) Metropolis-Hastings update. Ignored families. method character: "mcmc" (default, Bayesian MCMC fit) "als" (fast, MCMC-free point-estimation path). dynamic arguments, method = "als" uses pooled-static lame_als estimator. normal, binary, Poisson panels, method = "als" also supports dynamic penalized point-estimation path dynamic_ab, selected dynamic_beta, smooth AR(1) dynamic_uv directed, symmetric, bipartite panels, bipartite dynamic_G, Student-t dynamic_uv directed, symmetric, bipartite panels. snap-dynamic_uv = TRUE, dynamic_uv_kind = "snap" case routes lame_snap_als supported normal unipartite bipartite panels. Node-covariate coefficients use orthogonal additive-effect decomposition lame_als; dynamic node coefficients use period-specific node values selected dynamic_beta, static node coefficients use per-actor means. Named changing-composition panels aligned union actor set actor-entry gaps break dynamic smoothing penalties. Posterior draws, rank/censored families, bipartite snap-shift fits node covariates, dynamic coefficients, dynamic G_t handled snap ALS shortcut. als_stability character (used method = "als" dynamic ALS path selected): "none" (default), "quick", "validation". non-"none" presets rerun dynamic ALS fit jittered starts attach fit$stability. Smooth dynamic ALS fits report fitted-surface coefficient-path differences across starts; snap ALS fits report snap-score, snap-class, top-period, fitted-surface differences. als_max_iter positive integer (used method = "als"): maximum block-coordinate iterations ALS fit. Default 200. als_tol optional positive scalar (used method = "als"): convergence tolerance ALS objective fitted values. NULL keeps ALS estimator's built-default. bootstrap integer (used method = "als"): number bootstrap replicates. 0 (default) skips bootstrap; N > 0 runs N replicates attaches result confint returns bootstrap intervals. bootstrap_type character (used method = "als"): "parametric" (default) "block". bootstrap_block_length integer: block length block bootstrap. bootstrap_seed optional integer seed bootstrap. resume_from optional path checkpoint file produced previous lame(..., checkpoint_path = path) call. non-NULL, lame() short-circuits input parsing delegates lame_resume user-supplied overrides forwarded. Pass nscan = K request K additional stored draws continuation. Note: checkpoint_path max_seconds currently overridden resume (stripped re-evaluating saved call); open original lame() call need iterative checkpointing time budget chain resumes. Pass verbose = FALSE resume (sampling progress bar gated burn != 0 continuation forces burn = 0). Default NULL. print Deprecated. Use verbose instead.

title: AME model fitting routine for longitudinal relational data — lame
text: BETA posterior samples regression coefficients. 2-dimensional matrix [n_stored, p] coefficients static. dynamic_beta flags coefficient time-varying, BETA 3-dimensional array [n_stored, p, T] whose third dimension time period. Static coefficients still present values replicated across third dimension. coef(fit) collapses [p, T] posterior-mean matrix. Migration amen. amen::ame() BETA slot always 2-D. Scripts compute apply(fit$BETA, 2, mean) amen fit silently aggregate across periods run lame() fit dynamic_beta active (second margin coefficient index shapes; new third margin time). Use length(dim(fit$BETA)) detect shape, call coef(fit) returns [p, T] matrix either case. VC posterior samples variance parameters APM posterior mean additive row effects BPM posterior mean additive column effects b U posterior mean multiplicative row effects u. dynamic_uv=TRUE, 3D array (n x R x T) V posterior mean multiplicative column effects v (asymmetric case). dynamic_uv=TRUE, 3D array (n x R x T) UVPM posterior mean UV ULUPM posterior mean ULU (symmetric case) L posterior mean L (symmetric case) EZ estimate expectation Z matrix. mode = "bipartite", EZ list per-period matrices whose row/column dimnames currently NULL. Use names(fit$APM) / names(fit$BPM) (dimnames(fit$YPM[[t]])) recover row/column actor ordering, lexicographic sort input actor names ("r10" sorts "r2" unless zero-pad). YPM posterior mean Y (imputing missing values) GOF observed (first row) posterior predictive (remaining rows) values four goodness--fit statistics. See gof post-hoc computation gof_plot visualization. start_vals Final parameter values MCMC, can used input future model run. model.name Name model (provided)

title: AME model fitting routine for longitudinal relational data — lame
text: command provides posterior inference parameters AME models longitudinal relational data, assuming one eight possible data types/models. model supports unipartite networks (square adjacency matrices) bipartite networks (rectangular adjacency matrices distinct row column node sets) across multiple time points. Dynamic Effects Implementation: dynamic_uv dynamic_ab parameters enable time-varying latent representations autoregressive processes. extensions particularly useful understanding network structure evolves time. Dynamic Multiplicative Effects (dynamic_uv=TRUE): latent factors U V evolve according transition selected dynamic_uv_kind. default "ar1" model uses Gaussian AR(1) drift: $$U_{,k,t} = \rho_{uv} U_{,k,t-1} + \epsilon_{,k,t}$$ \(\epsilon_{,k,t} \sim N(0, \sigma_{uv}^2)\), indexes actors, k indexes latent dimensions, t indexes time. parameter \(\rho_{uv}\) controls temporal persistence (values near 1 indicate slow evolution). captures time-varying homophily, latent community structure, transitivity dynamics. "snap" transition uses mixture AR(1) drift discontinuous reset transitions, "t" uses heavy-tailed Student-t innovations represented local transition scales. MCMC path, snap t transitions supported unipartite directed symmetric models. method = "als", Student-t dynamic UV also supported bipartite normal, binary, Poisson panels, snap ALS supported normal unipartite bipartite panels, including named panels actors enter exit. Key references: Sewell & Chen (2015): Introduced dynamic latent space models actor-specific evolution rates Durante & Dunson (2014): Nonparametric Bayesian approach allowing flexible evolution network structure Hoff (2011): Hierarchical multilinear models providing theoretical foundation temporal dependencies Dynamic Additive Effects (dynamic_ab=TRUE): sender () receiver (b) effects evolve : $$a_{,t} = \rho_{ab} a_{,t-1} + \epsilon_{,t}$$ $$b_{,t} = \rho_{ab} b_{,t-1} + \eta_{,t}$$ \(\epsilon_{,t}, \eta_{,t} \sim N(0, \sigma_{ab}^2)\). models time-varying individual activity levels (outdegree) popularity (indegree). Dynamic additive effects useful sender activity receiver popularity change time rather staying fixed across whole panel. Prior Specification Dynamic Parameters: \(\rho_{uv}, \rho_{ab} \sim TruncNormal(mean, sd, 0, 1)\): Ensures stationarity AR(1) process \(\sigma_{uv}^2, \sigma_{ab}^2 \sim InverseGamma(shape, scale)\): Controls innovation variance Default priors (\(\rho_{uv}\) mean=0.9, \(\rho_{ab}\) mean=0.8) favor smooth evolution Adjust rho_*_mean closer 1 slower evolution, closer 0 rapid changes Computational Considerations: Dynamic effects increase computation roughly 30 50 percent per iteration Memory usage scales O(nRT) dynamic_uv, O(n*T) dynamic_ab C++ implementation substantially faster pure R loop Convergence diagnostics: Monitor rho sigma parameters carefully Effective sample sizes typically lower due temporal correlation Recommend burn >= 1000 nscan >= 20000 dynamic models Model Selection Guidelines: Use dynamic_uv dynamic_ab : Networks show clear temporal trends density clustering Individual node behavior changes systematically time Community structure evolves (merging, splitting, drift) Use dynamic_uv : Latent structure/communities change individual effects stable Focus evolving homophily clustering patterns Network shows structural reconfiguration time Use dynamic_ab : Individual heterogeneity varies overall structure stable Actors' activity/popularity changes observation period Focus individual-level temporal dynamics Bipartite Network Models: mode="bipartite", model handles rectangular adjacency matrices Y dimensions n_A x n_B, n_A n_B represent number row column nodes respectively. Static Bipartite Case: model uses separate latent factor matrices: U: n_A x R_row matrix row node latent positions V: n_B x R_col matrix column node latent positions G: R_row x R_col interaction matrix mapping latent spaces Multiplicative term: U G V' captures bipartite community structure Dynamic Bipartite Case: dynamic_uv=TRUE bipartite networks: $$U_{,k,t} = \rho_{uv} U_{,k,t-1} + \epsilon_{,k,t}$$ $$V_{j,k,t} = \rho_{uv} V_{j,k,t-1} + \eta_{j,k,t}$$ indexes row nodes, j indexes column nodes, k indexes latent dimensions. dynamic_G = TRUE bipartite interaction matrix \(G_t\) varies period (separate \(R_\text{row} \times R_\text{col}\) matrix every time slice) returned fit$G_cube. MCMC estimator samples \(G_t\) Carter-Kohn/FFBS update; method = "als" estimates penalized point path normal, binary, Poisson bipartite panels, including named changing-composition panels. marginal \(U_t G_t V_t'\) linear predictor identified object; individual \(G_t\) entries can shift equivalent rotations scalings \(U_t\) \(V_t\). dynamic_G = TRUE bipartite . Key Differences Unipartite Models: dyadic correlation (rho): Bipartite edges inherently directed Separate dimensions: R_row R_col can differ row/column spaces Rectangular structure: Network density patterns differ square matrices Community interpretation: Captures affiliation patterns node types Standard AME Model Types: following describes eight standard data types/models available: "normal": normal AME model. "tobit": tobit AME model censored continuous data. Values censored zero, appropriate non-negative continuous relational data. "binary": binary probit AME model. "ordinal": ordinal probit AME model. intercept identifiable model. "cbin": AME model censored binary data.  value 'odmax' specifies maximum number links row may . "frn": AME model fixed rank nomination networks. higher value rank indicates stronger relationship. value 'odmax' specifies maximum number links row may . "rrl": AME model based row ranks. appropriate relationships across rows directly comparable terms scale. intercept, row random effects row regression effects estimable model. "poisson": overdispersed Poisson AME model count data. latent variable represents log mean Poisson distribution.

title: AME model fitting routine for longitudinal relational data — lame
text: Cassy Dorff, Shahryar Minhas, Tosin Salau

title: AME model fitting routine for longitudinal relational data — lame
code: data(YX_bin_list) fit<-lame(YX_bin_list$Y,YX_bin_list$X,burn=5,nscan=5,odens=1,family="binary") #> Warning: `family` = "binary" but `Y` contains values other than 0/1. #> ℹ `Y` will be thresholded to `1 * (Y > 0)`; if you meant counts, use "poisson", #>   or "ordinal"/"normal" as appropriate. # you should run the Markov chain much longer than this  # \donttest{ ## Time-varying regression coefficients (dynamic_beta). ## Make every dyadic coefficient evolve as an AR(1): fit_dyn <- lame(YX_bin_list$Y, YX_bin_list$X,                 family = "binary", R = 0,                 nscan = 200, burn = 50, odens = 5,                 dynamic_beta = "dyad") #> Warning: `family` = "binary" but `Y` contains values other than 0/1. #> ℹ `Y` will be thresholded to `1 * (Y > 0)`; if you meant counts, use "poisson", #>   or "ordinal"/"normal" as appropriate. dim(fit_dyn$BETA)        # [n_stored, p, T] -- 3-D when dynamic #> [1] 40  4  4 coef(fit_dyn)            # [p, T] posterior-mean coefficient paths #>                  t1        t2        t3        t4 #> intercept 0.1199238 0.1199238 0.1199238 0.1199238 #> X1_dyad   0.7118277 0.7078791 0.6908902 0.6654124 #> X2_dyad   0.8650829 0.8748416 0.8417964 0.7950323 #> X3_dyad   1.0582930 1.0777578 1.0792037 0.9840359 confint(fit_dyn)         # per-period 95% credible intervals #>                     2.5%     97.5% #> intercept[t1] 0.07566298 0.1711694 #> X1_dyad[t1]   0.53743396 0.8624926 #> X2_dyad[t1]   0.62135516 1.0598551 #> X3_dyad[t1]   0.76479496 1.3208536 #> intercept[t2] 0.07566298 0.1711694 #> X1_dyad[t2]   0.50982957 0.8755414 #> X2_dyad[t2]   0.63062747 1.0549406 #> X3_dyad[t2]   0.77008591 1.2578084 #> intercept[t3] 0.07566298 0.1711694 #> X1_dyad[t3]   0.54407504 0.8382262 #> X2_dyad[t3]   0.65171342 0.9979983 #> X3_dyad[t3]   0.84688357 1.2833791 #> intercept[t4] 0.07566298 0.1711694 #> X1_dyad[t4]   0.51387021 0.7652479 #> X2_dyad[t4]   0.60842819 0.9169838 #> X3_dyad[t4]   0.75128416 1.1401657 summary(fit_dyn)         # prints a "Dynamic coefficients per period" block #>  #> === Longitudinal AME Model Summary === #>  #> Call: #> [1] "Y ~ dyad(X1, X2, X3) + a[i] + b[j] + rho*e[ji], family = 'binary'" #>  #> Time periods: 4  #> Family: binary  #> Mode: unipartite  #> Dynamic regression coefficients: enabled, kind = 'ar1' (rho_beta = dyad : 0.93) #>  #> Regression coefficients: #> ------------------------ #>           Estimate StdError z_value p_value CI_lower CI_upper     #> intercept     0.12    0.031   3.857       0    0.076    0.171 *** #> X1_dyad      0.694    0.095   7.335       0    0.526    0.822 *** #> X2_dyad      0.844    0.117   7.244       0    0.631        1 *** #> X3_dyad       1.05    0.145   7.265       0    0.782    1.242 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #> Note: stars are a visual hint from posterior mean / SD only; for inference use the credible intervals. #>  #> Dynamic coefficients per period: #> ------------------------------- #>            Mean   Min   Max Drift_pct Dynamic #> intercept 0.120 0.120 0.120     0.000       N #> X1_dyad   0.694 0.665 0.712     6.688       Y #> X2_dyad   0.844 0.795 0.875     9.454       Y #> X3_dyad   1.050 0.984 1.079     9.065       Y #>  #> Variance components: #> ------------------- #>     Estimate StdError #> va     0.839    0.321 #> cab    0.437    0.193 #> vb     0.646    0.245 #> rho    0.210    0.059 #> ve     1.000    0.000 #>   (va = sender, cab = sender-receiver covariance, vb = receiver, #>    rho = dyadic correlation, ve = residual variance) # }

what: Fast (MCMC-free) AME estimation for a longitudinal network — lame_als
title: Fast (MCMC-free) AME estimation for a longitudinal network — lame_als
text: Fits additive multiplicative effects (AME) model longitudinal (replicated) network iterative block coordinate descent, producing fast point estimate MCMC credible intervals. longitudinal counterpart ame_als; estimated effects (mu, beta, , b, U, V) static (pooled across time), non-dynamic lame fit. estimation algorithm adapts iterative block coordinate descent estimator Social Influence Regression model Hoff & Minhas (sir::sir_alsfit()) AME model; port adaptation, original lame methodology. See ame_als algorithm details.

title: Fast (MCMC-free) AME estimation for a longitudinal network — lame_als
code: lame_als(   Y,   Xdyad = NULL,   Xrow = NULL,   Xcol = NULL,   R = 0,   family = "normal",   mode = c("unipartite", "bipartite"),   symmetric = FALSE,   max_iter = 200,   tol = 1e-06,   lowrank_method = c("mm", "als", "hybrid"),   non_normal_method = c("irls", "transform"),   link = c("probit", "logit"),   linear_solver = c("eigen", "qr", "auto"),   multistart = c("none", "cheap", "full"),   bootstrap = 0L,   bootstrap_type = c("parametric", "block"),   bootstrap_block_length = 1L,   bootstrap_seed = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   seed = 6886 )

title: Fast (MCMC-free) AME estimation for a longitudinal network — lame_als
text: Y list T relational matrices, 3D array [n_row, n_col, T]. named list may changing actor composition; every slice must carry row column names actors can aligned union panel. Unnamed lists arrays treated positionally must one fixed layout. Xdyad list T dyadic covariate matrices/arrays, NULL. Xrow list T row/sender covariate matrices, NULL. Xcol list T column/receiver covariate matrices, NULL. R integer dimension multiplicative effects (default 0). covariate coefficients conditional choice. multiplicative term \(u_i'v_j\) flexible high-variance regressor can correlate dyadic covariates, estimated beta can shift – occasionally change sign – R increases. Comparing R = 0 fit useful check whether covariate story driven latent rank. family one "normal", "binary", "poisson". rank censoring families MCMC-. mode "unipartite" (square) "bipartite" (rectangular). symmetric logical; fit symmetric (undirected) model. Unipartite . max_iter maximum number block coordinate descent iterations (default 200). tol convergence tolerance relative change residual sum squares (default 1e-6). lowrank_method inner solver multiplicative (low-rank) block: "mm" (default) weighted majorise-minimise; "als" alternating least squares; "hybrid" runs keeps lower-objective result. "als"/"hybrid" converge faster "mm" strongly unbalanced longitudinal panels available directed bipartite models (symmetric fits always use "mm"). three minimise objective, point estimate unchanged balanced data. non_normal_method non-normal ALS families, "irls" (default binary/poisson) runs iteratively reweighted least squares, giving fast approximate GLM AME fit coefficients requested link scale (Poisson log, binary logit/probit). "transform" fits one fixed Gaussian working response (log(y+1) Poisson, rank-normal scores binary); coefficients uncalibrated working scale mainly useful direction/ranking checks. directed R > 0 IRLS fit uses hybrid low-rank solver internally IRLS weights unbalanced. Uncertainty either path comes bootstrap sandwich covariance. link link non_normal_method = "irls" binary family: "probit" (default; matches ame/lame) "logit". poisson always uses log link; ignored otherwise. linear_solver solver regression block: "eigen" (default) eigendecomposes normal equations; "qr" uses QR factorisation observed design, stable ill-conditioned covariates; "auto" picks "qr" design ill-conditioned full rank. give answer well-conditioned designs. multistart R > 0 (non-convex objective), "none" (default) fits single deterministic start; "cheap" (4 starts) "full" (8 starts) also try random low-rank starts keep lowest-SSE fit, warning starts reach materially different optima. Reproducible given seed; global RNG stream left unchanged. bootstrap integer: > 0, additionally run bootstrap replicates parametric block bootstrap (via ame_als_bootstrap) point fit attach result fit$bootstrap. downstream accessors (confint.ame_als, summary, print) surface bootstrap intervals instead anti-conservative sandwich Wald intervals. Default 0 (bootstrap) – bootstrap expensive, imposed user just wants quick fit. bootstrap_type character: "parametric" (default) "block" – bootstrap scheme use bootstrap > 0. bootstrap_block_length integer: block length block bootstrap; used bootstrap_type = "block". bootstrap_seed optional integer seed bootstrap (point fit uses seed). verbose logical; print progress (default TRUE). seed random seed (default 6886). block coordinate descent deterministic, point estimate reproducible regardless; argument retained API consistency ame.

code: # \donttest{ data(YX_bin_list) fit_pll <- lame_parallel(YX_bin_list$Y, Xdyad = YX_bin_list$X,                          family = "binary", n_chains = 2, cores = 1,                          nscan = 100, burn = 20, odens = 5,                          dynamic_beta = "dyad", verbose = FALSE) #>  #> ── Running 2 chains sequentially ── #>  #> Starting chain 1 (`lame()`) #> Warning: `family` = "binary" but `Y` contains values other than 0/1. #> ℹ `Y` will be thresholded to `1 * (Y > 0)`; if you meant counts, use "poisson", #>   or "ordinal"/"normal" as appropriate. #> Completed chain 1 #> Starting chain 2 (`lame()`) #> Warning: `family` = "binary" but `Y` contains values other than 0/1. #> ℹ `Y` will be thresholded to `1 * (Y > 0)`; if you meant counts, use "poisson", #>   or "ordinal"/"normal" as appropriate. #> Completed chain 2 #> Combining chains... #>  #> ── MCMC Convergence Diagnostics  #> Number of chains: 2 #> Samples per chain: "320, 320" #> Diagnostics cover regression coefficients and variance components. #> ! what: Resume a lame() MCMC run from a checkpoint — lame_resume
title: Resume a lame() MCMC run from a checkpoint — lame_resume
text: fit started lame(..., checkpoint_path = "X.rds") terminated early (either via max_seconds external interruption), continues chain recent checkpoint. implementation pragmatic: loads saved RNG state re-invokes lame() original call arguments. result fresh fit picks previous one left random-number stream; underlying MCMC counter restarts 1.

title: Resume a lame() MCMC run from a checkpoint — lame_resume
code: lame_resume(path, nscan_more = NULL, ..., .envir = NULL)

title: Resume a lame() MCMC run from a checkpoint — lame_resume
text: path Checkpoint file path (one passed checkpoint_path lame()). nscan_more Optional integer, new nscan continuation. NULL, original nscan used. ... Additional arguments forwarded lame() (override saved values). .envir Environment evaluate saved call's data arguments. Defaults caller's frame; used internally lame() forwards resume.

title: Resume a lame() MCMC run from a checkpoint — lame_resume
text: fitted lame object.

title: Resume a lame() MCMC run from a checkpoint — lame_resume
text: Equivalent consolidated entry point. lame(resume_from = path, ...) short-circuits function user-supplied overrides forwarded; pass nscan = K resume call request K additional stored draws. call shapes supported. lame_resume(path, ...) form safer nested calls: consolidated form re-evaluates saved lame() call parent.frame(), lame() frame whose required formal arguments (Y, Xdyad, etc.) supplied resume call. Prefer lame_resume(path, ...) calling inside functions non-global scopes. Use nscan_more = K override nscan value K continuation. arguments can overridden passing ....

title: Resume a lame() MCMC run from a checkpoint — lame_resume
code: # \donttest{ ck <- tempfile(fileext = ".rds") data(YX_bin_list) # short run with very-aggressive max_seconds to force early termination fit1 <- lame(YX_bin_list$Y, YX_bin_list$X, family = "binary", R = 0,              nscan = 5000, burn = 50, odens = 5,              checkpoint_path = ck, checkpoint_every = 50L,              max_seconds = 0.5, verbose = FALSE) #> Warning: `family` = "binary" but `Y` contains values other than 0/1. #> ℹ `Y` will be thresholded to `1 * (Y > 0)`; if you meant counts, use "poisson", #>   or "ordinal"/"normal" as appropriate. if (isTRUE(fit1$terminated_early)) {   fit2 <- lame_resume(ck, nscan_more = 200)   dim(fit2$BETA) } #> Warning: `family` = "binary" but `Y` contains values other than 0/1. #> ℹ `Y` will be thresholded to `1 * (Y > 0)`; if you meant counts, use "poisson", #>   or "ordinal"/"normal" as appropriate. #> [1] 40  4 # }

what: Fast approximate dynamic snap-shift AME estimator — lame_snap_als
title: Fast approximate dynamic snap-shift AME estimator — lame_snap_als
text: Fits fast point-estimator approximation dynamic snap-shift latent-factor model longitudinal normal-valued networks. targets drift-versus-reset transition estimand lame dynamic_uv = TRUE dynamic_uv_kind = "snap", returns ALS snap scores rather MCMC draws.

title: Fast approximate dynamic snap-shift AME estimator — lame_snap_als
code: lame_snap_als(   Y,   Xdyad = NULL,   Xrow = NULL,   Xcol = NULL,   R = 2L,   R_row = NULL,   R_col = NULL,   family = "normal",   mode = c("unipartite", "bipartite"),   symmetric = FALSE,   max_iter = 200L,   tol = 1e-06,   snap_kappa = 2,   snap_pi_prior = c(a = 1, b = 9),   snap_update = c("soft", "hard", "annealed"),   snap_damping = 0.7,   estimate_rho_uv = TRUE,   estimate_sigma_uv = TRUE,   rho_uv = NULL,   sigma_uv = NULL,   hyper_update = c("robust", "em"),   drift_quantile = 0.05,   drift_min_transitions = 50,   rho_prior_mean = 0.98,   rho_prior_weight = 25,   align = c("sequential", "global", "none"),   threshold = 0.5,   min_sigma = 1e-04,   sigma_floor_fraction = 0.75,   ridge = 1e-08,   snap_stability_tol = 0.05,   snap_convergence = c("quantile", "max", "classification"),   snap_delta_quantile = 0.95,   snap_class_change_tol = 0.005,   unstable_top_n = 10L,   stability = c("none", "quick", "validation"),   verbose = TRUE,   seed = 6886 )

title: Fast approximate dynamic snap-shift AME estimator — lame_snap_als
text: Y list relational matrices, 3D array. Unipartite fits use [n, n, T] panels. Bipartite fits use rectangular [n_row, n_col, T] panels. named lists, slices may changing actor composition; estimator pads union actor set treats absent actor-periods unobserved snap-ineligible. Xdyad optional list dyadic covariate matrices/arrays, NULL. Xrow, Xcol supported fast snap-shift estimator; pass NULL. Use lame method = "mcmc" node-covariate dynamic snap models. R positive integer latent rank. bipartite fits, used default R_row R_col. R_row, R_col positive integer latent ranks bipartite row column positions. Defaults R. family currently "normal". mode "unipartite" "bipartite". symmetric logical; TRUE, fit symmetric latent-factor approximation report one snap-probability matrix. max_iter maximum block-coordinate iterations. tol convergence tolerance approximate penalized objective. snap_kappa diffuse snap-prior standard deviation. snap_pi_prior length-two vector c(, b) beta prior used regularize snap rate. snap_update one "soft", "hard", "annealed". snap_damping scalar (0, 1]; damping applied soft snap-score updates. Values 1 mix new profiled score previous score reduce oscillation large panels. Ignored snap_update = "hard". estimate_rho_uv, estimate_sigma_uv logical flags updating AR drift persistence innovation scale. rho_uv, sigma_uv optional fixed/initial AR drift parameters. hyper_update one "robust" "em". default "robust" estimates drift hyperparameters lower tail transition innovations, prevents broad ruptures absorbed overly diffuse drift process. "em" uses untrimmed soft-classification moment update. drift_quantile lower-tail transition quantile used hyper_update = "robust" estimating smooth-drift hyperparameters. drift_min_transitions minimum effective number transitions retained lower-tail update. keeps default overfitting smooth-drift scale small panels. rho_prior_mean, rho_prior_weight weak regularization persistence estimate. defaults encode snap-shift model's intended persistent, low-innovation drift baseline. align initialization alignment mode; "global" aligns period pooled static ALS fit, "sequential" aligns period previous initialized period, "none" leaves per-period factors unaligned. threshold hard-classification threshold snap_class. min_sigma lower bound drift scale. sigma_floor_fraction robust hyperparameter updates, iterative drift scale fall fraction initial sigma_uv. prevents broad snap assignments collapsing smooth-drift variance min_sigma. ridge small ridge added latent-position normal equations. snap_stability_tol tolerance declaring soft snap scores stable. separate tol, tracks penalized objective. snap_convergence convergence criterion snap scores. "quantile" uses snap_delta_quantile absolute score changes plus class-change share; "max" uses worst actor-period score change; "classification" uses class-change stability. worst-case max always stored warned high. snap_delta_quantile quantile actor-period score changes used snap_convergence = "quantile". snap_class_change_tol maximum share eligible actor-periods whose snap_class may change iterations still declaring snap convergence. unstable_top_n number largest actor-period snap-score changes store convergence diagnostics. stability optional start-sensitivity preset. "none" runs one fit. "quick" "validation" rerun estimator additional seeds attach fit$stability snap-score, classification, top-period, fitted-surface comparisons. verbose logical; print progress. seed integer seed initialization perturbations.

title: Fast approximate dynamic snap-shift AME estimator — lame_snap_als
text: object class "lame_snap_als" dynamic latent positions, snap scores, fitted values, residuals, convergence diagnostics.

title: Fast approximate dynamic snap-shift AME estimator — lame_snap_als
text: longer panels, default align = "sequential" keeps period--period movement view. align = "global" can help short panels, longer panels can pull period back toward pooled static fit make early jumps look strong. fixed drift scale, pass rho_uv sigma_uv estimate_rho_uv = FALSE estimate_sigma_uv = FALSE. convergence output separates usual score summary worst moving actor-period. default, convergence uses 95th percentile score changes share class changes. final_max_snap_delta unstable_transitions still show largest local moves. Read snap_prob ALS snap score. useful rankings heuristic classifications; Bayesian posterior probability. bipartite mode, fitted multiplicative term \(U_t G V_t'\) one static interaction matrix \(G\). returned snap_prob matrix contains row-actor snap scores snap_prob_v contains column-actor snap scores. Bipartite snap ALS estimate node covariates, dynamic coefficients, dynamic \(G_t\); combinations need separate model.

title: Fast approximate dynamic snap-shift AME estimator — lame_snap_als
code: set.seed(1) Y_bip <- lapply(seq_len(3), function(t) {   m <- matrix(rnorm(6 * 5), 6, 5)   rownames(m) <- paste0("r", seq_len(6))   colnames(m) <- paste0("c", seq_len(5))   m }) fit_bip <- lame_snap_als(Y_bip, R = 1, mode = "bipartite",                          max_iter = 3, verbose = FALSE) dim(fit_bip$snap_prob_v) #> [1] 5 3

title: Extract latent positions as a tidy data frame — latent_positions

Dynamic fits use time labels input; static (cross-sectional) fits return "1" every row downstream filtering time behaves cases. value Numeric. posterior mean latent position. posterior_sd Numeric. Posterior standard deviation latent position, NA posterior samples available. enable, fit model posterior_opts = posterior_options(save_UV = TRUE). type Character. "U" sender/row positions, "V" receiver/column positions. Symmetric models "U". Returns zero-row data frame correct column names R = 0.

title: Exact rolling-origin leave-future-out cross-validation — lfo
text: fitted lame object \(T\) periods, refits model first \(t - 1\) periods (\(t\) periods) computes expected log predictive density (elpd) period \(t\) refit. Returns summed elpd across leave-periods, along per-period contributions.

title: Exact rolling-origin leave-future-out cross-validation — lfo
code: lfo(fit, periods = NULL, refit = TRUE, ...)

title: Exact rolling-origin leave-future-out cross-validation — lfo
text: fit fitted lame object. periods Integer vector leave-periods evaluate. Default last 3 periods (tail(seq_len(T), 3L)). period \(t\) must satisfy \(t \ge 2\). refit Logical; TRUE (default), refits training window. FALSE, uses original posterior means (much rougher approximation). ... Passed refit lame() call (typically nscan, burn, odens, verbose).

title: Exact rolling-origin leave-future-out cross-validation — lfo
text: list elpd_lfo (total summed elpd), pointwise (per-dyad log-density leave-period; list numeric vectors, one per period — unlist(pointwise) gives flat vector suitable loo::loo_compare()-style stacking), p_lfo (effective number parameters), per_period (data frame period, elpd, n_obs), periods (periods evaluated).

title: Exact rolling-origin leave-future-out cross-validation — lfo
code: # \donttest{ data(YX_bin_list) fit <- lame(YX_bin_list$Y, YX_bin_list$X, family = "binary", R = 0,             dynamic_beta = "dyad",             nscan = 200, burn = 50, odens = 5, verbose = FALSE) #> Warning: `family` = "binary" but `Y` contains values other than 0/1. #> ℹ `Y` will be thresholded to `1 * (Y > 0)`; if you meant counts, use "poisson", #>   or "ordinal"/"normal" as appropriate. lfo_res <- lfo(fit, periods = c(3L, 4L), refit = TRUE,                nscan = 100, burn = 20, odens = 5, verbose = FALSE) #> Warning: ! Posterior 95% interval for `rho_beta` reaches 0.991. #> ℹ Under AR(1) the `h`-step forecast variance saturates at the stationary level #>   `sigma_beta^2 / (1 - rho_beta^2)`, which itself explodes as `rho_beta -> 1`; #>   horizons beyond "3" become uninformative in this regime. #> ℹ Consider refitting with `dynamic_beta_kind = "rw1"` if the underlying process #>   is genuinely unit-root. #> Warning: ! Posterior 95% interval for `rho_beta` reaches 0.991. #> ℹ Under AR(1) the `h`-step forecast variance saturates at the stationary level #>   `sigma_beta^2 / (1 - rho_beta^2)`, which itself explodes as `rho_beta -> 1`; #>   horizons beyond "3" become uninformative in this regime. #> ℹ Consider refitting with `dynamic_beta_kind = "rw1"` if the underlying process #>   is genuinely unit-root. print(lfo_res) #>  #> ── Exact rolling-origin LFO ── #>  #> Periods evaluated: 3 and 4 #> Refit per leave-out: TRUE #> Total elpd_lfo: -18316 #>  period       elpd n_obs #>       3  -8047.719  2450 #>       4 -10268.440  2450 # }

what: ERGM-style covariate helpers for ame() / lame() — nodematch
title: ERGM-style covariate helpers for ame() / lame() — nodematch
text: nodematch(x) returns n x n matrix 1 x[] == x[j] 0 otherwise – AME analogue ERGM's nodematch term, suitable Xdyad single homophily covariate. absdiff(x) returns absolute difference |x[] - x[j]|, AME analogue ERGM's absdiff. nodefactor(x) returns dyadic matrix x[] + x[j] numeric x (list per-level binary dyadic indicators factor / character x).

title: ERGM-style covariate helpers for ame() / lame() — nodematch
code: nodematch(x, na_diag = TRUE)  absdiff(x, na_diag = TRUE)  nodefactor(x, na_diag = TRUE)

title: ERGM-style covariate helpers for ame() / lame() — nodematch
text: x numeric, factor, character vector length n. na_diag logical: set diagonal NA? Defaults TRUE.

title: ERGM-style covariate helpers for ame() / lame() — nodematch
text: n x n numeric matrix (, factor x passed nodefactor, named list matrices).

title: ERGM-style covariate helpers for ame() / lame() — nodematch
text: helpers return matrix list matrices ready wrap n x n x p Xdyad array via simplify2array array().

title: ERGM-style covariate helpers for ame() / lame() — nodematch
code: # \donttest{ n <- 12 grp <- sample(letters[1:3], n, replace = TRUE) age <- rnorm(n, 40, 10) # build a single same-group homophily covariate Xdyad <- array(nodematch(grp), dim = c(n, n, 1),                dimnames = list(NULL, NULL, "same_group")) # combine homophily + age difference Xdyad <- array(c(nodematch(grp), absdiff(age)),                dim = c(n, n, 2),                dimnames = list(NULL, NULL, c("same_group", "age_diff"))) # }

title: Post-MCMC per-actor time-varying slopes — per_actor_slopes
text: Computes smoothed per-actor time-varying slope coefficient nodal covariate slice. kind = "row", row-actor \(\) gets length-\(T\) slope path \(\beta_{,t}\) Xrow[, covariate_idx, t], fit ridge-penalised least squares residual main MCMC linear predictor.

title: Post-MCMC per-actor time-varying slopes — per_actor_slopes
code: per_actor_slopes(fit, kind = c("row", "col"), covariate_idx = 1L, lambda = 1)

title: Post-MCMC per-actor time-varying slopes — per_actor_slopes
text: fit fitted lame object. kind "row" (default; per-row-actor slopes) "col" (per-column-actor slopes). covariate_idx Integer column index chosen nodal covariate (Xrow Xcol). Default 1L. lambda Non-negative smoothing parameter (first-difference penalty across periods). Default 1.

title: Post-MCMC per-actor time-varying slopes — per_actor_slopes
text: list slopes (\(n_{actors} \times T\) matrix), kind, covariate_idx, lambda, label. Class "per_actor_slopes".

title: Post-MCMC per-actor time-varying slopes — per_actor_slopes
code: # \donttest{ data(YX_bin_list) fit <- lame(YX_bin_list$Y, YX_bin_list$X, family = "binary", R = 0,             nscan = 100, burn = 25, odens = 5, verbose = FALSE) #> Warning: `family` = "binary" but `Y` contains values other than 0/1. #> ℹ `Y` will be thresholded to `1 * (Y > 0)`; if you meant counts, use "poisson", #>   or "ordinal"/"normal" as appropriate. # post-MCMC per-row-actor slopes on the first dyadic covariate pas <- per_actor_slopes(fit, kind = "row", lambda = 1) dim(pas$slopes) #> [1] 50  4 # }

code: # S3 method for class 'lame' plot(   x,   which = c(1, 2, 3, 4),   time.points = NULL,   ask = FALSE,   pages = c("single", "multiple"),   ... )

title: Plot diagnostics for a LAME model fit — plot.lame
text: x object class "lame" fitting LAME model numeric character vector specifying plots produce: 1 "trace" = MCMC trace plots, 2 "density" = posterior density plots, 3 "gof" = longitudinal goodness--fit plots, 4 "effects" = additive multiplicative effects, 5 "network" = network snapshots selected times. Default c(1,2,3,4) show main diagnostic plots. time.points numeric vector time points network snapshots (used "network" ). Default c(1, middle, last). ask logical; TRUE, user prompted plot page pages character string specifying arrange plots: "single" = one combined page (default), "multiple" = separate pages plot type ... additional arguments (currently used)

title: Plot diagnostics for a LAME model fit — plot.lame
text: NULL (invisibly). Plots displayed side effects.

title: Plot diagnostics for a LAME model fit — plot.lame
text: function produces multi-panel plot containing: MCMC trace plots Shows mixing convergence key parameters Posterior distributions Density plots regression coefficients variance components Longitudinal GOF Time series observed network statistics posterior predictive intervals Effects time Evolution additive effects across time periods (applicable) Network snapshots Visualization network selected time points plot adapts longitudinal structure: Shows temporal trends network statistics Highlights composition changes actors enter/exit Displays credible intervals time-varying statistics

title: Plot diagnostics for a LAME model fit — plot.lame
text: Cassy Dorff, Shahryar Minhas, Tosin Salau

title: Plot diagnostics for a LAME model fit — plot.lame
code: # \donttest{ # Create simple longitudinal network data set.seed(6886) n <- 10 nms <- paste0("n", 1:n) Y_list <- list(   matrix(rnorm(n * n), n, n, dimnames = list(nms, nms)),   matrix(rnorm(n * n), n, n, dimnames = list(nms, nms)) ) diag(Y_list[[1]]) <- diag(Y_list[[2]]) <- NA fit <- lame(Y_list, family = "normal",             nscan = 50, burn = 10, odens = 1, verbose = FALSE, plot = FALSE)  # default combined plot plot(fit) #> ℹ Generating LAME diagnostic plots: trace, density, gof, effects #> ℹ Combining plots into single page layout  # }

what: Predict method for LAME models — predict.lame
title: Predict method for LAME models — predict.lame
text: Generate predictions fitted longitudinal AME model. Returns list matrices (one per time point) requested scale.

title: Predict method for LAME models — predict.lame
code: # S3 method for class 'lame' predict(   object,   newdata = NULL,   type = c("response", "link"),   h = 0L,   by_draw = FALSE,   interval = c("none", "credible"),   probs = c(0.025, 0.975),   newexposure = NULL,   ... )

title: Predict method for LAME models — predict.lame
text: object Fitted LAME model object. newdata Optional list T dyadic covariate arrays ([n_row, n_col, p] , actors fit) compute counterfactual predictions. NULL, training-data predictions returned. type Character; "response" (default) "link". h Integer >= 0: forecast horizon. h = 0 (default), returns -sample predictions . h > 0, propagates AR(1) (RW1) state-space model forward h periods returns list h matrices (one per future period). Requires least one dynamic component fit. Warns posterior \(\rho_\beta\) near 1. by_draw TRUE h > 0, returns n x n x h x n_draws array per-draw forecasts instead per-period means. interval One "none" (default) "credible". h > 0 "credible", per-period output list length-3 lists $lower, $median, $upper matrices computed probs quantiles across posterior draws. Ignored -sample (h = 0) predictions. probs Length-2 vector lower / upper quantiles credible interval interval = "credible". Default c(0.025, 0.975). newexposure Optional length-h non-negative numeric vector future-period exposures (Poisson ). omitted fit period_exposure stored, defaults last observed exposure; absent, defaults 1. ... Additional arguments (used).

title: Predict method for LAME models — predict.lame
text: List prediction matrices (one per time point).

what: Long-format draws of the linear predictor for marginaleffects-style use — prediction_draws_long
title: Long-format draws of the linear predictor for marginaleffects-style use — prediction_draws_long
text: Returns long-format data frame one row per (draw, , j, period) combination, giving per-draw linear predictor (response-scale prediction) dyad period. Intended marginaleffects- / tidybayes-style downstream summarisation: column names follow .draw / .chain / .iteration / .value convention tidybayes::spread_draws() marginaleffects::posterior_draws() auto-dispatch returned data frame. Actor period names fit$Y's dimnames carried forward actor_i, actor_j, period_label columns.

title: Long-format draws of the linear predictor for marginaleffects-style use — prediction_draws_long

title: Long-format draws of the linear predictor for marginaleffects-style use — prediction_draws_long
text: long-format data frame columns .chain, .iteration, .draw, period, period_label, , j, actor_i, actor_j, .value. Returned tibble tibble package available; otherwise plain data frame.

what: Procrustes alignment of latent positions across time — procrustes_align
title: Procrustes alignment of latent positions across time — procrustes_align
text: Aligns dynamic latent positions across time periods remove arbitrary rotational indeterminacy. essential interpreting temporal trajectories latent positions, since latent space identified rotation time point. Uses Procrustes rotation: time step t, finds orthogonal rotation matrix best aligns U_t U_t-1 (minimizing Frobenius norm), applies sequentially t=2 T.

title: Procrustes alignment of latent positions across time — procrustes_align
code: procrustes_align(   object = NULL,   U = NULL,   V = NULL,   G = NULL,   return_fit = FALSE,   per_draw = FALSE,   ... )

title: Procrustes alignment of latent positions across time — procrustes_align
text: object fitted ame lame object, NULL raw arrays provided via U/V/G. U Optional 3D array [n, R, T] sender latent positions. object provided U NULL, extracted object$U. V Optional 3D array [n, R, T] receiver latent positions. unipartite asymmetric models, aligned independently. bipartite models, aligned jointly U via G interaction matrix. G Optional [R_row, R_col] interaction matrix bipartite models, transformed preserve invariant U G V'. return_fit Logical. TRUE object provided, returns modified copy fit object aligned latent positions. Default FALSE. per_draw Logical. TRUE, run Procrustes alignment per posterior draw rather posterior-mean trajectory. uses object$U_full / object$V_full (per-draw posterior cubes — populated posterior_opts includes storing U/V) present; otherwise falls back mean-trajectory alignment emits informational note. Per-draw alignment methodologically correct treatment — rotation indeterminacy per-draw property, per-mean one — memory-hungry. Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments (currently unused).

title: Procrustes alignment of latent positions across time — procrustes_align
text: return_fit = FALSE (default): list components U (aligned sender positions), V (aligned receiver positions, applicable), G (updated interaction matrix, bipartite). return_fit = TRUE: copy object aligned latent positions replacing originals.

title: Procrustes alignment of latent positions across time — procrustes_align
text: unipartite networks, U V aligned independently using separate Procrustes rotations. symmetric networks, U present aligned. bipartite networks, U V aligned jointly: separate rotation matrices computed U V, G interaction matrix updated G_aligned = t(R_U) %*% G %*% R_V preserve product U %*% G %*% t(V). U 2D matrix (static model single time point), returned unchanged informational message.

title: Procrustes alignment of latent positions across time — procrustes_align
text: Cassy Dorff, Shahryar Minhas, Tosin Salau

title: Procrustes alignment of latent positions across time — procrustes_align

title: Gibbs update for additive effects covariance — rSab_fc
text: Gibbs sampling covariance matrix additive row column effects AME model. function implements inverse-Wishart posterior update covariance matrix Sab.

title: Gibbs update for additive effects covariance — rSab_fc
code: rSab_fc(a, b, Sab0=NULL, eta0=NULL, rvar=TRUE, cvar=TRUE, symmetric=FALSE)

title: Gibbs update for additive effects covariance — rSab_fc
text: vector row random effects (additive sender effects) b vector column random effects (additive receiver effects) Sab0 prior (inverse) scale matrix prior distribution. Default diag(2), provides weakly informative prior. eta0 prior degrees freedom prior distribution. Default 4, minimum proper prior 2x2 matrix. rvar logical: row variance updated? (default TRUE) cvar logical: column variance updated? (default TRUE) symmetric logical: symmetric network? (default FALSE)

title: Gibbs update for additive effects covariance — rSab_fc
text: Updated covariance matrix Sab (2x2 matrix variances diagonal covariance -diagonal)

title: Gibbs update for additive effects covariance — rSab_fc
text: function implements different update strategies: Full update: rvar cvar TRUE, updates full 2x2 covariance matrix using inverse-Wishart distribution Row variance : rvar TRUE, updates Sab[1,1] Column variance : cvar TRUE, updates Sab[2,2] Symmetric case: symmetric TRUE, enforces equal variances high correlation (0.999) row column effects

title: Gibbs update for additive effects covariance — rSab_fc
text: Peter Hoff, Shahryar Minhas

what: Gibbs update for multiplicative effects covariance — rSuv_fc
title: Gibbs update for multiplicative effects covariance — rSuv_fc
text: Gibbs sampling covariance matrix multiplicative effects U V AME model. function implements inverse-Wishart posterior update covariance matrix stacked UV effects.

title: Gibbs update for multiplicative effects covariance — rSuv_fc
code: rSuv_fc(U, V, Suv0=NULL, kappa0=NULL)

title: Gibbs update for multiplicative effects covariance — rSuv_fc
text: U matrix multiplicative row effects (n x R matrix n number nodes R dimension multiplicative effects) V matrix multiplicative column effects (n x R matrix) Suv0 prior (inverse) scale matrix prior distribution. Default identity matrix dimension 2R x 2R, providing weakly informative prior. kappa0 prior degrees freedom prior distribution. Default 2 + 2R, minimum proper prior 2R x 2R covariance matrix.

title: Gibbs update for multiplicative effects covariance — rSuv_fc
text: Updated covariance matrix Suv (2R x 2R matrix) stacked effects [U, V]. first R x R block contains covariances U, last R x R block contains covariances V, -diagonal blocks contain cross-covariances U V.

title: Gibbs update for multiplicative effects covariance — rSuv_fc
text: function updates full covariance matrix multiplicative effects using inverse-Wishart distribution. posterior distribution : $$Suv ~ IW(kappa0 * Suv0 + t(UV) \%*\% UV, n + kappa0)$$ UV = cbind(U, V) stacked matrix effects. hierarchical prior allows adaptive shrinkage multiplicative effects, amount shrinkage determined data posterior update.

title: Gibbs update for multiplicative effects covariance — rSuv_fc
text: Peter Hoff, Shahryar Minhas

what: Multivariate split-R-hat for dynamic_beta coefficient paths — rhat_dynamic_beta
title: Multivariate split-R-hat for dynamic_beta coefficient paths — rhat_dynamic_beta
text: Given one multi-chain lame fits whose $BETA [n_iter, p, T], computes multivariate R-hat per coefficient \(k\) treating length-\(T\) path one multivariate observation per iteration.

title: Multivariate split-R-hat for dynamic_beta coefficient paths — rhat_dynamic_beta
code: rhat_dynamic_beta(fit_list, coefs = NULL)

title: Multivariate split-R-hat for dynamic_beta coefficient paths — rhat_dynamic_beta
text: fit_list list fitted lame objects lame_parallel(..., chains = K), ame_chain_list produced re-running lame() different seeds. coefs Optional character vector coefficient names subset (matches dimnames(fit$BETA)[[2]]).

title: Multivariate split-R-hat for dynamic_beta coefficient paths — rhat_dynamic_beta
text: Data frame one row per coefficient: coef, rhat_mvt, rhat_max_univariate, n_chains, n_iter_per_chain, T. rhat_mvt Brooks-Gelman multivariate statistic; rhat_max_univariate max per-(k,t) split-R-hat values comparison.

title: Multivariate split-R-hat for dynamic_beta coefficient paths — rhat_dynamic_beta
text: chain \(c\), let \(\beta^{(c)}_t \\mathbb{R}^T\) path. \(m\) chains \(n\) iterations per chain, define $$W_k = \tfrac{1}{m}\sum_c S_c^{(k)}, \quad         B_k = \tfrac{n}{m-1}\sum_c (\bar\beta_c^{(k)} - \bar\beta^{(k)})(\bar\beta_c^{(k)} - \bar\beta^{(k)})'$$ \(S_c^{(k)}\) within-chain sample covariance path \(k\) chain \(c\). \(V_k = ((n-1)/n) W_k + ((m+1)/(mn)) B_k\) \(\hat R_k^{mvt} = \sqrt{\lambda_\max(W_k^{-1} V_k)}\). nearly degenerate covariances add tiny ridge \(W_k\).

title: Multivariate split-R-hat for dynamic_beta coefficient paths — rhat_dynamic_beta
code: # \donttest{ data(YX_bin_list) fit_list <- lapply(c(1L, 2L, 3L, 4L), function(s) {   set.seed(s)   lame(YX_bin_list$Y, YX_bin_list$X, family = "binary", R = 0,        dynamic_beta = "dyad",        nscan = 200, burn = 50, odens = 5, verbose = FALSE) }) #> Warning: `family` = "binary" but `Y` contains values other than 0/1. #> ℹ `Y` will be thresholded to `1 * (Y > 0)`; if you meant counts, use "poisson", #>   or "ordinal"/"normal" as appropriate. #> Warning: `family` = "binary" but `Y` contains values other than 0/1. #> ℹ `Y` will be thresholded to `1 * (Y > 0)`; if you meant counts, use "poisson", #>   or "ordinal"/"normal" as appropriate. #> Warning: `family` = "binary" but `Y` contains values other than 0/1. #> ℹ `Y` will be thresholded to `1 * (Y > 0)`; if you meant counts, use "poisson", #>   or "ordinal"/"normal" as appropriate. #> Warning: `family` = "binary" but `Y` contains values other than 0/1. #> ℹ `Y` will be thresholded to `1 * (Y > 0)`; if you meant counts, use "poisson", #>   or "ordinal"/"normal" as appropriate. rhat_dynamic_beta(fit_list) #>        coef  rhat_mvt rhat_max_univariate n_chains n_iter_per_chain T #> 1 intercept 0.9874209            1.237604        4               40 4 #> 2   X1_dyad 0.9874209            2.235824        4               40 4 #> 3   X2_dyad 0.9874209            1.945086        4               40 4 #> 4   X3_dyad 0.9874209            2.070859        4               40 4 # }

what: Cowles MH update for explicit ordinal cutpoints (Z-marginalised) — sample_alpha_cowles
title: Cowles MH update for explicit ordinal cutpoints (Z-marginalised) — sample_alpha_cowles
text: One Metropolis-Hastings sweep alpha Z integrated conditional. proposal symmetric random walk delta = log(diff(alpha)) space; acceptance ratio uses marginal ordinal-probit likelihood. Mixing alpha update dramatically faster data-induced Gibbs convention (Cowles 1996 reports ESS gains order magnitude standard ordinal-probit benchmarks).

code: sample_sigma_G2(   vecG_path,   rho_G,   prior_shape = 2,   prior_rate = 1,   s2_obs = 1,   v_cap_mult = 4 )

title: Inverse-gamma posterior draw for sigma_G^2 given the FFBS path — sample_sigma_G2
text: s2_obs observation-noise variance (1 probit/binary). v_cap_mult cap stationary G-state variance units s2_obs (default 4).

title: Inverse-gamma posterior draw for sigma_G^2 given the FFBS path — sample_sigma_G2
text: Scale identification. model identifies product \(U_t G_t V_t'\), \(G_t\) alone, overall scale vec(G_t) free: left unchecked chain finds degenerate mode sigma_G^2 (hence G_t) inflates orders magnitude U,V collapse compensate, leaving linear predictor unchanged reported G_cube meaningless. Clamping rho_G alone bound stationary state variance sigma_G^2 / (1 - rho_G^2). therefore cap implied stationary variance v_cap_mult * s2_obs (observation-noise units), forces multiplicative scale onto U,V – regularising N(0, s2) prior pins – keeps G_t scale comparable static-G fit. cap loose enough never bind genuinely small-variation G_t.

title: Simulate networks from a fitted AME model — simulate.ame
text: Generates multiple network realizations fitted AME model. function performs conditional posterior predictive simulation: draws stored MCMC samples available uses posterior means latent components retained. Unlike print.ame, displays fitted quantities, simulate.ame draws new networks posterior predictive distribution.

title: Simulate networks from a fitted AME model — simulate.ame
code: # S3 method for class 'ame' simulate(   object,   nsim = 100,   seed = NULL,   newdata = NULL,   burn_in = 0,   thin = 1,   return_latent = FALSE,   ... )

title: Simulate networks from a fitted AME model — simulate.ame
text: object fitted model object class "ame" nsim number networks simulate (default: 100) seed random seed reproducibility newdata optional list containing new covariate data: Xdyad dyadic covariates (n x n x p array nA x nB x p bipartite) Xrow row/sender covariates (n x p matrix nA x p bipartite) Xcol column/receiver covariates (n x p matrix nB x p bipartite) NULL, uses covariates original model fit burn_in number initial MCMC samples discard (default: 0, assumes burn-already removed) thin thinning interval MCMC samples (default: 1, use every sample) return_latent logical: return latent Z matrices addition Y? (default: FALSE) ... additional arguments (currently used)

title: Simulate networks from a fitted AME model — simulate.ame
text: list components: Y list nsim simulated networks format original data Z return_latent=TRUE, list nsim latent Z matrices family family model (binary, normal, etc.) mode network mode (unipartite bipartite)

title: Simulate networks from a fitted AME model — simulate.ame
text: Model: AME model represents networks latent variable framework: $$Y_{ij} \sim F(Z_{ij})$$ F observation model (e.g., probit binary) Z latent network: $$Z_{ij} = \beta^T x_{ij} + a_i + b_j + u_i^T v_j + \epsilon_{ij}$$ Components: \(\beta\): regression coefficients dyadic/nodal covariates \(a_i, b_j\): additive sender receiver random effects \(u_i, v_j\): multiplicative latent factors (dimension R) \(\epsilon_{ij}\): dyadic random effects correlation \(\rho\) Simulation: simulated network k = 1, ..., nsim: Parameter draw: Draw parameter set \(\theta^{(k)}\) MCMC chains: Sample iteration s uniformly stored MCMC samples Extract \(\beta^{(s)}\), variance components \((v_a^{(s)}, v_b^{(s)}, v_e^{(s)}, \rho^{(s)})\) Random effects: Sample new random effects posterior distributions: \(a_i^{(k)} \sim N(0, v_a^{(s)})\) = 1, ..., n (row effects) \(b_j^{(k)} \sim N(0, v_b^{(s)})\) j = 1, ..., m (column effects) Fresh draws posterior variance carry random-effect uncertainty simulated networks Latent network: Build expected latent positions: $$E[Z_{ij}^{(k)}] = \beta^{(s)T} x_{ij} + a_i^{(k)} + b_j^{(k)} + \hat{u}_i^T \hat{v}_j$$ \(\hat{u}_i, \hat{v}_j\) posterior mean latent factors Dyadic correlation: Add correlated noise structure: $$Z_{ij}^{(k)} = E[Z_{ij}^{(k)}] + \epsilon_{ij}^{(k)}$$ \(\epsilon\) covariance structure: $$Cov(\epsilon_{ij}, \epsilon_{ji}) = \rho^{(s)} v_e^{(s)}$$ $$Var(\epsilon_{ij}) = v_e^{(s)}$$ Observation model: Generate observed network: Binary: \(Y_{ij}^{(k)} = (Z_{ij}^{(k)} > 0)\) Normal: \(Y_{ij}^{(k)} = Z_{ij}^{(k)}\) Poisson: \(Y_{ij}^{(k)} \sim Poisson(\exp(Z_{ij}^{(k)}))\) families use appropriate link functions Sources uncertainty: simulation captures three types uncertainty: Parameter uncertainty: Different MCMC samples yield different \(\beta, v_a, v_b, v_e, \rho\) Random effect uncertainty: Fresh draws \(N(0, v_a), N(0, v_b)\) simulation Dyadic uncertainty: Correlated random noise \(\epsilon_{ij}\) resulting simulations propagate uncertainty stored parameter draws fresh dyadic/random-effect draws. Latent components stored MCMC draws held posterior means. Latent-factor draws: Multiplicative effects (U, V) use posterior means unless fit retained compatible latent-factor draws. Storing full latent-factor chains can require substantial additional memory. Symmetric Networks: symmetric networks, model enforces \(a_i = b_i\) \(u_i = v_i\), latent matrix Z symmetrized generating observations.

title: Simulate networks from a fitted AME model — simulate.ame
text: Cassy Dorff, Shahryar Minhas, Tosin Salau

title: Simulate networks from a fitted AME model — simulate.ame
code: # \donttest{ # Fit a model data(YX_bin) fit <- ame(YX_bin$Y, Xdyad = YX_bin$X, burn = 10, nscan = 100, odens = 1,            family = "binary", verbose = FALSE)  # Simulate 10 networks from posterior sims <- simulate(fit, nsim = 10) # }

title: Simulate longitudinal networks from a fitted LAME model — simulate.lame
text: Mathematical Framework Longitudinal Networks: LAME model extends AME multiple time periods T potential temporal dependencies. time t = 1, ..., T: $$Y_{ij,t} \sim F(Z_{ij,t})$$ latent network evolves : $$Z_{ij,t} = \beta^T x_{ij,t} + a_{,t} + b_{j,t} + u_{,t}^T v_{j,t} + \epsilon_{ij,t}$$ Temporal Dynamics: LAME can incorporate three types temporal dependencies: Static Effects: Parameters constant time \(a_{,t} = a_i\), \(b_{j,t} = b_j\) t \(u_{,t} = u_i\), \(v_{j,t} = v_j\) t Dynamic Additive Effects: AR(1) process random effects $$a_{,t} = \rho_{ab} a_{,t-1} + \eta_{,t}, \quad \eta_{,t} \sim N(0, \sigma_a^2(1-\rho_{ab}^2))$$ $$b_{j,t} = \rho_{ab} b_{j,t-1} + \xi_{j,t}, \quad \xi_{j,t} \sim N(0, \sigma_b^2(1-\rho_{ab}^2))$$ \(\rho_{ab}\) temporal correlation parameter Dynamic Multiplicative Effects: AR(1) latent factors $$u_{,t} = \rho_{uv} u_{,t-1} + \omega_{,t}$$ $$v_{j,t} = \rho_{uv} v_{j,t-1} + \psi_{j,t}$$ Uncertainty Quantification Process Trajectories: simulated trajectory k = 1, ..., nsim: Step 1: Parameter Sampling Draw MCMC iteration s uniformly stored posterior samples Extract static parameters: \(\beta^{(s)}\), variance components Extract temporal parameters applicable: \(\rho_{ab}^{(s)}\), \(\rho_{uv}^{(s)}\) Step 2: Initialize t = 1 Depending start_from parameter: "posterior": Use posterior means starting values "random": Draw stationary distribution additive effects: \(a_{,1}^{(k)} \sim N(0, \sigma_a^2)\) multiplicative effects: Initialize prior Step 3: Evolve Time t = 2, ..., T: ) Update Dynamic Effects (applicable): $$a_{,t}^{(k)} = \rho_{ab}^{(s)} a_{,t-1}^{(k)} + \eta_{,t}^{(k)}$$ \(\eta_{,t}^{(k)} \sim N(0, \sigma_a^2(1-[\rho_{ab}^{(s)}]^2))\) innovation variance \(\sigma_a^2(1-\rho_{ab}^2)\) ensures stationarity b) Construct Latent Network: $$E[Z_{ij,t}^{(k)}] = \beta^{(s)T} x_{ij,t} + a_{,t}^{(k)} + b_{j,t}^{(k)} + u_{,t}^T v_{j,t}$$ c) Add Dyadic Noise: $$Z_{ij,t}^{(k)} = E[Z_{ij,t}^{(k)}] + \epsilon_{ij,t}^{(k)}$$ correlation structure preserved AME model d) Generate Observations: Apply appropriate link function based family Sources Uncertainty Longitudinal Context: Cross-sectional uncertainty (AME): Parameter uncertainty MCMC Random effect variability Dyadic noise Temporal uncertainty: Uncertainty temporal correlation parameters \(\rho_{ab}, \rho_{uv}\) Innovation noise AR(1) processes Propagation uncertainty time (compounds periods) Initial condition uncertainty: Different starting values lead different trajectories Captured start_from options Interpretation Multiple Trajectories: simulated trajectory represents one possible evolution network conditional stored fit. Variation across trajectories captures: Model parameter uncertainty Stochastic variation temporal evolution Accumulated uncertainty time periods ensemble trajectories provides prediction intervals widen time, reflecting increasing uncertainty longer-term forecasts. Special Considerations: Temporal Correlation: Higher \(\rho\) values create smoother trajectories persistence Stationarity: AR(1) innovation variance scaled maintain stationary marginal distributions Missing Time Points: simulating beyond observed data (n_time > T_observed), covariates recycled set zero appropriate warnings Limitations: simulate.ame, multiplicative effects use posterior means unless fit retained compatible latent-factor draws. Storing complete MCMC chains \(u_{,t}, v_{j,t}\) time points memory-intensive large networks long time series.

title: Simulate longitudinal networks from a fitted LAME model — simulate.lame
text: Shahryar Minhas

title: Simulate longitudinal networks from a fitted LAME model — simulate.lame
code: # \donttest{ # Create simple longitudinal network data set.seed(1) n <- 10 nms <- paste0("n", 1:n) Y_list <- list(   matrix(rnorm(n * n), n, n, dimnames = list(nms, nms)),   matrix(rnorm(n * n), n, n, dimnames = list(nms, nms)) ) diag(Y_list[[1]]) <- diag(Y_list[[2]]) <- NA fit <- lame(Y_list, family = "normal",             nscan = 50, burn = 10, odens = 1, verbose = FALSE, plot = FALSE)  # Simulate 10 network trajectories from posterior sims <- simulate(fit, nsim = 10) # }

title: MCMC trace plots and density plots for AME/LAME model parameters — trace_plot
text: function produces two types diagnostic plots: Trace plots Show evolution parameter values across MCMC iterations. Good mixing indicated rapid exploration parameter space trends stuck periods. Density plots Show posterior distribution parameters. Multiple modes may indicate identification issues convergence problems. plots help diagnose: Convergence: chain reached stationary distribution? Mixing: chain exploring parameter space efficiently? Autocorrelation: successive samples highly correlated? Parameters displayed include: Regression coefficients (beta) Variance components (va, vb, cab, rho, ve)

code: # \donttest{ data(YX_bin_list) fit <- lame(YX_bin_list$Y, YX_bin_list$X, family = "binary",             burn = 5, nscan = 20, odens = 1, verbose = FALSE) #> Warning: `family` = "binary" but `Y` contains values other than 0/1. #> ℹ `Y` will be thresholded to `1 * (Y > 0)`; if you meant counts, use "poisson", #>   or "ordinal"/"normal" as appropriate. # toggle dynamic_beta on without rewriting the whole call fit_dyn <- update(fit, dynamic_beta = "dyad") #> Warning: `family` = "binary" but `Y` contains values other than 0/1. #> ℹ `Y` will be thresholded to `1 * (Y > 0)`; if you meant counts, use "poisson", #>   or "ordinal"/"normal" as appropriate. dim(fit_dyn$BETA)  # 3-D now #> [1] 20  4  4 # }

what: Update an ame_als / lame_als fit — update.ame_als
title: Update an ame_als / lame_als fit — update.ame_als
text: S3 method update re-fits ALS fit modified arguments. Two routes: ... contains warm-start refit knobs (Y_new, X_new, Z_new, max_iter, tol, verbose), delegates ame_als_refit fast warm-started refit; otherwise (e.g. changing R, family, lambda, bootstrap) re-evaluates original object$call overrides, matching update.ame semantics MCMC side. split exists ame_als_refit() genuinely warm-started single-fit refit (faster, narrower argument set), changing R / family requires cold-start refit ame_als.

title: Update an ame_als / lame_als fit — update.ame_als
code: # \donttest{ Y <- matrix(rnorm(400), 20, 20); diag(Y) <- NA fit <- ame_als(Y, R = 1, family = "normal", verbose = FALSE) # warm-start refit with the same arguments (fast) fit_w <- update(fit, max_iter = 50) # cold-start refit with a different R (full restart) fit_R2 <- update(fit, R = 2) # }

title: Visualize multiplicative effects (latent factors) from AME models — uv_plot
text: fit object class "ame" "lame" containing multiplicative effects, network matrix Y U V provided separately Y Network matrix (needed fit provided) U Matrix sender latent factors (extracted fit provided) V Matrix receiver latent factors (extracted fit provided) row.names Names row nodes (defaults rownames Y U) col.names Names column nodes (defaults colnames Y V) layout Character string specifying layout: "circle" (default) "biplot" vscale Scaling factor V positions relative U (default 0.8) show.edges Logical; TRUE, show network edges (default FALSE) edge.alpha Transparency edges (default 0.3) node.size Size nodes, "degree" scale degree (default 3) label.nodes Logical; TRUE, show node labels (default TRUE) label.size Size node labels (default 3) show.usernames Integer: number top-degree nodes label, NULL default behavior sender.color Color sender/row nodes (default "darkred") receiver.color Color receiver/column nodes (default "darkblue") colors Optional vector colors nodes (e.g., communities) title Optional title plot time_point dynamic UV, time point plot (default: last). Can numeric index "average" time-averaged positions plot_type dynamic UV: "snapshot" (single time), "trajectory" (evolution), "faceted" (grid time points). static UV, ignored. show_arrows trajectory plots, whether show directional arrows highlight Optional character vector actor names highlight plot_type = "trajectory" plot. Highlighted actors coloured colour-blind-safe Okabe-Ito palette; actors rendered grey lower alpha. Ignored static snapshot plots.

text: covariance matrix c(intercept, dyadic coefficients), matching row/column names.

title: Sandwich covariance for the regression coefficients of a fast AME fit — vcov.ame_als
text: estimate conditional sandwich \(B^{-} M B^{-}\) bread \(B = D'WD\) (\(D\) observed intercept + dyadic-covariate design, \(W\) fit's observation weights) meat \(M\) heteroskedasticity-robust (cluster = "none", HC0 meat) dyad-clustered (cluster = "dyad", default) outer product weighted score contributions \(w_\ell e_\ell d_\ell\). normal transform fit weights unit, reduces ordinary \(D'D\) sandwich; IRLS fit uses final IRLS weights, matching estimating equation fit actually solved. Dyad clustering pools score across \((,j)\), \((j,)\) time, reflects dyadic dependence (reciprocity, repeated observation). conditional: additive effects , b multiplicative term held fixed, omits estimation uncertainty anti-conservative. Node-covariate, additive multiplicative standard errors returned – use ame_als_bootstrap fully-propagated inference.

title: lame 1.2.0
text: Cleaned ALS support documentation.

title: lame 1.1.0
text: Fast, MCMC-free estimation via ame_als() lame_als(): alternating least squares / IRLS point estimator normal, binary, Poisson families. Supports parametric-bootstrap sandwich standard errors full S3 method set (coef(), vcov(), confint(), predict(), tidy(), glance(), diagnostic plots).

title: lame 1.0.0
text: Cross-sectional network analysis via ame() support 8 distributional families: normal, binary, ordinal, tobit, Poisson, censored binary, fixed rank nomination, row-ranked likelihood. Longitudinal network analysis via lame() dynamic additive multiplicative effects modeled AR(1) processes. Support unipartite (square) bipartite (rectangular) network structures. Covariate support: dyadic (Xdyad), row (Xrow), column (Xcol) covariates automatic design matrix construction. S3 methods: print(), summary(), coef(), vcov(), confint(), predict(), fitted(), residuals(), simulate(), plot(). Visualization functions: trace_plot(), gof_plot(), ab_plot(), uv_plot() MCMC diagnostics model assessment. Goodness--fit via posterior predictive checks gof(). C++ acceleration via Rcpp RcppArmadillo core sampling routines. Handles changing actor compositions across time periods.
